title: 10 best herbal creams for stomach growling

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, alleviating stomach growling and discomfort.

Liquiritin: This flavonoid has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by digestive acid and other irritants, reducing the likelihood of stomach growling and discomfort.

Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and demulcent effects, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation that may contribute to stomach growling.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with stomach growling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound glycyrrhizin in licorice root soothes the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause growling. The cream also helps to calm the digestive system, regulating the balance of stomach acids and enzymes. This leads to a reduction in stomach contractions, resulting in a decrease in growling sensations. Additionally, licorice creams may also help to alleviate digestive discomfort and promote a sense of calmness.
remedy_constituents: Licorice saponins: These triterpenoid saponins help with stomach growling by reducing inflammation in the stomach and promoting the healing of gastric mucosa, which can help alleviate symptoms of stomach discomfort.

Glycyrrhizin: As a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, glycyrrhizin helps to soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to stomach growling and discomfort.

Flavonoids (including liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin): These plant-derived flavonoids possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to calm the stomach and alleviate symptoms of stomach growling, such as bloating and cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach issues.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes are also used as they contain a similar composition to roots, providing anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe stomach discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves are occasionally used in combination with roots or rhizomes, as they are believed to have a synergistic effect in alleviating stomach problems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier to ensure quality and potency.
Grind the glycyrrhiza glabra roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 50 grams of the ground glycyrrhiza glabra powder with 50 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring constantly, until the beeswax is fully melted.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring it to a storage container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with stomach growling because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the digestive system. Additionally, ginger's natural warming properties can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, promoting a sense of comfort and relief from stomach growling. remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols are a class of terpenes found in Zingiber officinale that help with stomach growling by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate nausea and discomfort.
Shogaols: Shogaols are another type of terpene in Zingiber officinale that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce stomach pain and discomfort associated with stomach growling.

Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, are phenolic compounds in Zingiber officinale that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce stomach inflammation and discomfort caused by stomach growling.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These are the thick, underground stems that contain the highest concentration of active compounds, which help to alleviate stomach issues.

Roots: The roots of the plant have been traditionally used to create digestive aid creams due to their ability to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Extract 50g of dried zingiber officinale rhizomes using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio.
Combine the extracted zingiber officinale with 100g of distilled water and heat the mixture to 60-70°c for 30 minutes.
Mix 20g of emulsifying wax with 30g of beeswax and 15g of coconut oil to create a thickening agent.
Combine the thickening agent with the zingiber officinale mixture and stir in 20g of vitamin e oil and 10g of shea butter.
Filter the cream through a cheesecloth or fine mesh and store it in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedy_parts: Barks: Barks are commonly used to make teas and other infusions for digestive issues, as they contain compounds that may help soothe stomach discomfort.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes (or rhizomes) are the underground stems of the plant and may be used to make teas or infusions that have anti-inflammatory properties, which could help alleviate stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried cinnamomum verum bark in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the infusion to obtain 1 cup of cinnamomum verum decoction and let it cool to room temperature.
Mix 1/2 cup of distilled water with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 1/4 cup of the cooled cinnamomum verum decoction to the melted beeswax mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a 6-ounce jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried foeniculum vulgare root and 50g of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds from a trusted supplier.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine the ground foeniculum vulgare root powder with 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 20 minutes or until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully incorporated.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the resulting herbal foeniculum vulgare cream in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh centaurea cyanus flowers and dry them thoroughly for 24 hours.
Combine the dried flowers with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated then remove from heat.
Let the mixture cool completely before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Transfer the cream to sterilized jars filled with 2 inches of air space and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

Crush 50g of dried sideritis scardica leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the crushed leaf powder with 100ml of cold-pressed olive oil in a glass jar, allowing it to infuse for 24 hours at room temperature.

remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, making them an ideal choice for medicinal creams targeting stomach growling.
Buds: Bell pepper buds contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of stomach growling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum peppers and wash them thoroughly with water.
Dry the peppers in a warm oven at 150°f for 6-8 hours or air dry for 7 days.
Grind the dried peppers into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a mesh strainer to remove lumps.
Mix the pepper powder with coconut oil in a ratio of 1:2 and add 5 drops of peppermint essential oil and stir well until smooth.
Store the cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months and apply topically as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with stomach growling because of its unique properties. The cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by indigestion and bloating. Additionally, the damask rose's astringent and antispasmodic properties calm down muscle spasms in the stomach, which can contribute to growling sounds. By promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort, herbal damask rose creams can effectively alleviate stomach growling symptoms, leaving you feeling more comfortable and at ease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Damask rose cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help soothe and calm digestive issues such as stomach discomfort, bloating, and cramps associated with hunger pangs.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content in damask rose creams can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and potentially alleviating stomach growling sensations.

Gastric-soothing compounds: Damask rose contains a compound called rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have gastric-protective effects, helping to calm the digestive system and reduce symptoms like stomach growling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe digestive issues and calm stomach discomfort.
Seeds: Containing omega-6 fatty acids, seeds provide a natural anti-inflammatory effect that can aid in reducing stomach acidity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried rosa centifolia flowers and rinse them gently with cold water.
Combine the rinsed flowers with 1 cup of olive oil in a glass jar and store it for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 1 tablespoon of beeswax, 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until it reaches 180°f, stirring occasionally for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Juniper creams helps with stomach growling because of its natural antispasmodic properties that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The cream's active compounds, such as juniper oil, work to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing cramps and spasms that can cause uncomfortable rumbling sounds. By easing digestion and relieving discomfort, juniper creams provide fast-acting relief from stomach growling, leaving you feeling more at ease and confident throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Juniper essential oil has a carminative effect, which helps to expel gas from the digestive system and relieve bloating and discomfort associated with stomach growling.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Juniper has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the gut lining, which can contribute to stomach discomfort and growling sensations.

Digestive stimulant properties: The essential oil of juniper berries has been traditionally used to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and abdominal pain, which can be accompanied by stomach growling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Juniper roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming stomach discomfort.
Buds: Juniper buds contain antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and ease digestive issues associated with stomach growling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh juniper berries and dry them thoroughly in a warm place for at least 7 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried juniper berries with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 4 hours, stirring occasionally, to create a decoction and infuse the oil with juniper extract.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container and discard the solids after 30 minutes of pressing time.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 10 drops of lavender essential oil, then melt in a double boiler for about 20 minutes to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry creams helps with stomach growling because of its unique blend of herbal extracts that soothe and calm digestive issues. The highbush cranberry, rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, works to reduce inflammation and irritation in the gut, while other herbs like ginger and peppermint ease cramps and spasms. This combination helps to quiet stomach growls by promoting a healthy balance of digestion, reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of fullness and satisfaction. As a result, individuals can enjoy relief from nagging hunger pangs and feel more at ease throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Highbush cranberry contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating stomach growling caused by inflammatory conditions.

Digestive enzymes and probiotics: The cream may contain digestive enzymes and probiotics that can aid digestion, break down nutrients, and regulate gut bacteria, helping to alleviate stomach discomfort and growling.

Antispasmodic properties: Highbush cranberry's antispasmodic compounds, such as myricetin and quercetin, can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that may contribute to stomach growling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the highbush cranberry plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including stomach discomfort and indigestion.
Barks: The bark of the highbush cranberry plant contains a compound called proanthocyanidin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe stomach upset.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vaccinium macrocarpon berries in the summer months weighing about 250 grams.
Dry the harvested berries in a warm oven at 150°f for 3 hours or until crispy.
Grind the dried berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder and measure out 30 grams.
Mix the ground vaccinium macrocarpon with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for at least 2 hours before transferring it to airtight containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with stomach growling because of its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The active compounds in yarrow, such as acetic acid and chamazulene, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining. This helps to alleviate symptoms associated with indigestion and bloating, allowing for a smoother digestion process. Additionally, yarrow creams can also help to increase digestive enzyme production, further supporting the body's natural ability to break down food efficiently.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Yarrow cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to stomach growling.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of yarrow cream help to relax smooth muscle contractions in the stomach and intestines, which can alleviate stomach cramps and discomfort that may cause growling.

Digestive stimulant property: Yarrow cream's digestive stimulant properties can help to increase digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can reduce feelings of hunger and bloating that can contribute to stomach growling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm stomach discomfort.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds contain a compound called apiol, which has natural antispasmodic effects, helping to reduce digestive cramps and gas.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry the achillea millefolium flowers in a clean and well-ventilated area for at least 24 hours.
Combine one part dried achillea millefolium flowers with two parts coconut oil and infuse the mixture in a dark glass jar for 6 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Mix the strained oil with one part beeswax and heat the mixture over low heat in a double boiler until the wax melts and forms a smooth consistency.
Pour the cream into small tin containers, let it cool to room temperature, and store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with stomach growling because they contain the herb angelica, which has a calming effect on the digestive system. The cream works to soothe the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation, helping to quiet the rumbling and discomfort associated with stomach growling. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of angelica help to reduce symptoms of bloating and indigestion, providing relief from uncomfortable stomach noises and feelings of fullness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Angelica creams contain antispasmodic compounds that help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing stomach contractions and cramps that can lead to growling and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Angelica creams have anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which can alleviate symptoms of stomach growling such as bloating, discomfort, and indigestion.
Carminative properties: Angelica creams contain carminative compounds that help expel gas from the digestive tract, reducing the pressure and discomfort that can cause stomach growling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help soothe digestive issues and calm stomach growling.
Rhyzomes: Contains angelic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with stomach growling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves for a minimum of 2 ounces dried material.
Combine 2 ounces dried angelica archangelica roots and leaves with 4 cups water in a large saucepan.
Simmer the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain and discard solids to yield 2 cups liquid extract.
Mix 1 tablespoon beeswax with 8 tablespoons coconut oil and heat until melted, adding 2 cups liquid extract.
Stir the mixture vigorously until cooled and thickened, filling glass jars and labeling accurately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with stomach growling because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe digestive issues. The herb's natural astringent properties help to reduce excess mucus in the stomach, calming any irritation or discomfort that may be contributing to the rumbling noise. Additionally, stinging nettle has been shown to improve gut health by promoting digestion and reducing inflammation, which can lead to a decrease in stomach growling. By addressing these underlying issues, stinging nettle creams can provide relief from embarrassing and uncomfortable stomach noises.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that may help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with stomach growling.

Antioxidant properties: Nettle creams contain antioxidants that can help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to stomach growling and other digestive issues.

Gastroprotective properties: Stinging nettle has been traditionally used to protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, which may help prevent irritation and inflammation in the stomach and intestines, reducing the occurrence of stomach growling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm digestive issues, making them an effective ingredient for relieving stomach growling.
Barks: Containing compounds like triterpenes and phenolic acids, the barks of stinging nettle help to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh nettle leaves and dry them in the shade for 7-10 days to ensure potency retention.
Grind 15g of dried nettle leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Combine the ground nettle powder with 30ml of sweet almond oil and 5ml of beeswax in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Stir the mixture constantly over low heat until the beeswax melts completely and the mixture reaches a temperature of 65-70°c for 20 minutes.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized containers, label them, and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or in the freezer for up to a year.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for stomach growling are topical remedies that help soothe and calm stomach discomfort through the application of herbs directly to the skin. These creams typically contain ingredients like ginger, peppermint, and chamomile, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and calming properties. Examples include creams like GingerEase and Peppermint Calm, which can help alleviate stomach growling by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. This can improve lives by providing an alternative to oral medication and promoting overall digestive well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stomach growling that are treated with herbal creams are skin irritations, such as eczema and acne, which can be alleviated by topical application of calming herbs like aloe vera and chamomile. Additionally, itching caused by allergies or insect bites can be soothed by creams containing herbs like calendula and licorice root, while wound healing can be enhanced with creams containing herbs like St. John's Wort and plantain.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with stomach growling is a blend of Peppermint, Ginger, and Fennel creams. Peppermint helps to calm the digestive system and alleviate nausea, while Ginger aids in digestion and reduces inflammation. Fennel, with its carminative properties, helps to release trapped gas and alleviate bloating. Applying a mixture of these creams topically can provide soothing relief from stomach discomfort and help to quiet growling sensations. Regular application may also aid in long-term digestive health.
